h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I can put author name from the page heading and the speaker slot as speaker or voice in the poem if the line evidence is only “I.” The named listener box is still the one I’m checking right at submission, because a blank looks risky even if

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues refining and confirming the same rule about condition-column labeling.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: So the role fields come from the condition column: plain water is control, fertilizer is treatment. The height number belongs in measured outcome, even if the fertilizer row is taller or shorter. I think I can submit it that way, but I want to check: if fertilizer had a lower height, it would still be treatment because the added condition stays the label source?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly applies the condition-first labeling rule and tests a counterfactual, but this is consolidation rather than a qualitatively new device or task; the criterion was already taught.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal of fortune; the tutoring moves proceed steadily without the tutor needing to change route or invent a new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, and the tutor continues the same framing move without adapting to a learner re-framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner correctly applies the condition-first labeling rule and tests a counterfactual, but this is consolidation rather than a qualitatively new device or task; the criterion was already taught.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, and the tutor continues the same framing move without adapting to a learner re-framing.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will correctly label the top and bottom numbers and understand that speed is not represented.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Top number: beat count. Bottom number: beat unit. No box names speed. [draws the bar line] One bar ends after the third quarter-note beat.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the labeling and drawing task as directed, but does not independently initiate a new device or criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner pressure triggers a reversal; the tutor's route is consistent and effective.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Force on the cart by the floor. That arrow is the floor contact on the cart, not the rolling. Earth on cart would be a separate card, down.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was moving toward a clean separation of contact forces per acting object, with L2 confidently distinguishing floor-contact from Earth-down. L3 introduces an unresolved decomposition question.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Does the floor card here mean normal force only, or does friction need a separate floor-on-cart arrow?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 poses a productive analytic question that could lead to a new classification criterion, but does not yet perform or complete the separation into normal and friction arrows.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No tutor turn after L3 exists in the transcript, so no stock-taking or new mechanism can appear; the scene ends on the learner's pressure without a tutor response.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the pivot L3, so no uptake adapting to the learner's revised framing can be scored.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Force on the cart by the floor. That arrow is the floor contact on the cart, not the rolling. Earth on cart would be a separate card, down.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: L3 poses a productive analytic question that could lead to a new classification criterion, but does not yet perform or complete the separation into normal and friction arrows.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no later tutor turn after the pivot L3, so no uptake adapting to the learner's revised framing can be scored.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No tutor turn after L3 exists in the transcript, so no stock-taking or new mechanism can appear; the scene ends on the learner's pressure without a tutor response.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No tutor turn after L3 exists in the transcript, so no stock-taking or new mechanism can appear; the scene ends on the learner's pressure without a tutor response.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues applying the header/value/note classification to the next column.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: So if the header is just `rainfall`, then `millimeters` cannot stay in the header slot. I think it has to go in the handoff note, maybe as `rainfall is measured in millimeters for each row`.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 correctly performs the boundary-case test for a new header, demonstrating application of the classification device to a novel variation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Thin does not mean empty. It means the header names the field and may carry reading metadata; the handoff note is still the later sentence. ... Boundary test: if the header were only `rainfall`, where would `millimeters` have to move?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 first takes stock of the learner's hesitation as a boundary case and then switches the task from classifying given labels to a constructed test: removing the unit tag and redistributing the information, making the learner do different work.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No later tutor turn exists after the pivot L4; the transcript ends at L4.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: So if the header is just `rainfall`, then `millimeters` cannot stay in the header slot. I think it has to go in the handoff note, maybe as `rainfall is measured in millimeters for each row`.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: L4 correctly performs the boundary-case test for a new header, demonstrating application of the classification device to a novel variation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No later tutor turn exists after the pivot L4; the transcript ends at L4.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T3 first takes stock of the learner's hesitation as a boundary case and then switches the task from classifying given labels to a constructed test: removing the unit tag and redistributing the information, making the learner do different work.
